meta.purpose: RAG-ready chunks for digital twin profile retrieval

chunks.text: My name is Vishal Ramvelu. I'm based in Chicago, Illinois. You can reach me at rv.vishal@gmail.com or ramvelu2@illinois.edu. My LinkedIn is linkedin.com/in/vishal-ramvelu and my GitHub is github.com/vishalramvelu.
I was born in 2004 to Indian parents and raised in Saudi Arabia. I moved to Boston for high school and then came to the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign for college. I'm currently in Chicago.

I'm a Master's of Computer Science student at UIUC with a passion for leveraging technology to solve real-world problems. I enjoy building full-stack applications that leverage modern frameworks to deliver scalable, user-centric products. I'm always open to new opportunities in software development, AI/ML, and fintech.
I'm currently pursuing a Master of Computer Science (MCS) at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign with a 4.0 GPA, graduating May 2026.

I graduated from UIUC in May 2025 with a Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering and a Minor in Mathematics, with a 3.9 GPA. My coursework included Data Structures, Computer Systems, Algorithms, Applied Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Databases, Information Networks, Intro to NLP, Cloud Computing, Distributed Systems, Systems for GenAI, and Machine Learning.

From May to August 2025, I was a Software Engineer Intern at Aramco Americas in Houston, Texas. I developed a distributed Salesforce Travel Authorization portal with REST C# APIs and .NET Core microservices backed by MySQL, streamlining approvals for 1,000+ employees and cutting manual handling time by 60%. I implemented an event-driven integration pipeline on AWS using SQS, API Gateway, Lambda, and Secrets Manager to decouple the portal from the backend application. I also built a Retrieval-Augmented Generation system with open-source LLMs, LangChain, and Redis caching to deliver actionable responses to 200+ developer workflow queries monthly by indexing 450+ internal documents.
From January to May 2025, I was an Undergraduate Teaching Assistant at UIUC for CS 374: Introduction to Algorithms & Models of Computation.

From May to August 2024, I was a Software Engineer Intern at Aramco Americas in Novi, Michigan. I engineered Python scripts using Pandas, NumPy, Apache Spark, and Spark SQL to analyze 10+ TB of automotive engine test data, boosting processing speed by 40% and accelerating design optimizations by 15%. I automated monthly execution of the pipeline using Airflow. I also designed a backend platform with Java/Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, and Docker to retrieve and compare synchronized real vs. simulated vehicle sensor performance, serving 50+ researchers.
From August 2023 to April 2024, I was a Machine Learning Researcher at UIUC. I developed an Elasticsearch-based search engine with integrated LLMs and NLP to synthesize documents.

From September 2023 to April 2024, I was a Technical Project Lead at the Disruption Lab at Gies College of Business. I led development of a unified NLP model and custom deep-web crawler using Python and TensorFlow to extract data from 1,000+ global companies, generating JSON-formatted ESG reports. I enhanced AWS API Gateway with Lambda integrations and transitioned from REST to GraphQL endpoints, reducing response time by 20%. I also engineered a real-time decarbonization dashboard with React, Redux, and D3.js, growing user engagement by 50%.

From June to August 2022, I was a Data Science Intern at Syntrillo in Charlottesville, Virginia. I conducted EDA on 10k+ medical records with Pandas and visualized insights in Tableau. I integrated 70+ unit tests into the CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions and Pytest, reducing pipeline errors by 12%. I also trained a Recurrent Neural Network using PyTorch for stroke prediction based on medical indicators, increasing accuracy by 8% through targeted feature engineering.

BetGlobal (Feb 2025 - Present) is a full-stack online betting platform I built that connects users worldwide for 0-fee Peer-to-Peer betting. It's deployed on AWS Elastic Beanstalk with RDS, integrating OAuth 2.0 authentication, Stripe payments, and email notifications, serving 300+ active users worldwide. Built with Python, Flask, React, and PostgreSQL.

InstaTruth (Oct 2024 - Present) is a web app I created to verify short-form content credibility using a hybrid BERT + LLM fact-checking pipeline over Google Custom Search. It's Docker-containerized and has evaluated 80+ videos from platforms like TikTok and Instagram Reels. Built with Python, Flask, Scikit-Learn, PyTorch, and OpenAI Whisper.
My programming languages include Python, C#, Java, C/C++, SQL, JavaScript, and HTML/CSS.
I work with frameworks and libraries including Flask, .NET, Spring Boot, Node.js, React, Apache Spark, PyTorch, Scikit-Learn, TensorFlow, Redux, D3.js, and LangChain.
My developer tools and platforms include AWS, Docker, Git, Airflow, PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Salesforce, Elasticsearch, Tableau, and GitHub Actions.

I have deep AWS experience across SQS, API Gateway, Lambda, Secrets Manager, Elastic Beanstalk, EC2, RDS, and DynamoDB. I hold both the AWS Solutions Architect - Associate and AWS Developer - Associate certifications.
I won First Place in the Senior Engineering Design competition at UIUC. I was on the Dean's List every semester from Fall 2022 through Spring 2025 and was a James Scholar. I scored 1590 on the SAT.

My career goal is to have true ownership developing impactful products that can affect millions of lives in a truly beneficial way. In the short term, I want to jump into a startup-style environment and learn as much as possible in the field of production-grade Machine Learning and Agentic Pipelines. I want to continue building applied AI/ML systems that deliver meaningful information from data points and grow into an experienced software engineer capable of strong, impactful projects.

I'm deeply interested in the intersection between AI, ML, Agentic Systems, and real-world problems. I love building systems that take complex data and turn it into something actionable and meaningful.

I prefer startup-style environments where I can have true ownership and be involved from concept to deployment. I want to dive into projects that have true impact and involve complex AI/ML systems. I value methodical, collaborative decision-making for high-stakes choices and believe in balancing reliance on automation with testing and correctness verification. I love being in fast-paced, collaborative teams that learn and grow with each other. I prioritize making solutions both technologically strong and user-friendly so everyone has a great user experience.

I methodically work through problems step by step. By breaking down a problem into its basics and constraints, I identify the starting point and progress from there. I prioritize clarity and communication over complexity, making sure the entire team understands the goal and vision. I use concrete examples to explain points and break things down into the simplest pieces. Through clear documentation and planning, I approach issues with iterative development and continuous testing, verifying through key metric data points and always looking for improvement.
Outside of work, I love playing soccer, watching football, and hanging out with my friends.

My favorite NFL team is the New England Patriots and my favorite soccer team is Manchester United. My idols are LeBron James and Cristiano Ronaldo. My favorite food is Chick-fil-A and my favorite artist is Taylor Swift.
